sections.paragraph:
ἐπισύστασις, εως, ἡ a gathering together against established authority, uprising, disturbance, insurrection (σύστασις ‘bringing together’; Sext. Emp., Eth. 127; Berosus: 680 Fgm. 9 [ in Jos., C. Ap. 1, 149]; SIG 708, 27 [I b.c.]; PEnteux 86, 5 [221 b.c.]; Num 17:5; 26:9; 1 Esdr 5:70; Artapanus: 726 Fgm. 2 [in Eus., PE 9, 23, 1] Jac.) Ac 24:12 v.l. (for ἐπίστασις). On 2 Cor 11:28 v.l. s. ἐπίστασις.—DELG s.v. ἵστημι. M-M.

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Berosus Berosus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PEnteux PEnteux = Papyrus Enteuxeis—List 4

Artapanus Artapanus , II b.c.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 383
